title: Verses, Supposed To Be Written By Alexander Selkirk, During His Solitary Abode In The Island Of Juan Fernandez.
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I am monarch of all I survey,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My right there is none to dispute;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From the centre all round to the sea
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I am lord of the fowl and the brute.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O Solitude! where are the charms
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That sages have seen in thy face?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Better dwell in the midst of alarms
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Than reign in this horrible place.


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I am out of humanityן¿½s reach,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I must finish my journey alone,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Never hear the sweet music of speech,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I start at the sound of my own.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The beasts, that roam over the plain,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My form with indifference see;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ They are so unacquainted with man,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Their tameness is shocking to me.


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Society, friendship, and love,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Divinely bestowן¿½d upon man,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O, had I the wings of a dove,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How soon would I taste you again!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My sorrows I then might assuage
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In the ways of religion and truth,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Might learn from the wisdom of age,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And be cheerן¿½d by the sallies of youth.


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Religion! what treasure untold
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Resides in that heavenly word!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ More precious than silver and gold
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or all that this earth can afford.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But the sound of the church-going bell
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ These valleys and rocks never heard,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Never sighן¿½d at the sound of a knell,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or smiled when a Sabbath appearן¿½d.


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ye winds, that have made me your sport,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Convey to this desolate shore
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Some cordial endearing report
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of a land I shall visit no more.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My friends, do they now and then send
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A wish or a thought after me?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O tell me I yet have a friend,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Though a friend I am never to see.


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How fleet is the glance of the mind!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Compared with the speed of its flight,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The tempest itself lags behind,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And the swift-winged arrows of light.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When I think of my own native land,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In a moment I seem to be there;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But alas! recollection at hand
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Soon hurries me back to despair.


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But the sea-fowl is gone to her nest,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The beast is laid down in his lair;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Even here is a season of rest,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And I to my cabin repair.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Thereן¿½s mercy in every place,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And mercy, encouraging thought!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gives even affliction a grace,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And reconciles man to his lot.
meaning:
"Verses, Supposed To Be Written By Alexander Selkirk, During His Solitary Abode In The Island Of Juan Fernandez." is a quintessential example of William Cowper's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
